dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 3 for a suspected Stroke/CVA at a residence in Richmond.
Hi, I'm Mark, an EMT with the rescue squad. What led to the call today?
She has a migraine and is having trouble speaking.
My words feel stuck. It's very hard to get them out.
Hello Mary, can you hear me?
Can you open your eyes for me please?
(nods head)
Can you tell me your name?
Mary... Mary Smith.
Can you squeeze my fingers if you're comfortable?
(squeezes fingers slowly)
Ma'am, we'll now be checking your breathing-can you breathe normally for a moment?
Mary, just keep breathing naturally as we check respiration.
Respirations appear to be at 18, and are regular and unlabored.

dialogue.micro_intent: Oxygen saturation measured with pulse oximetry
dialogue.utterance: Oxygen saturation is 98%-not dropping or fluctuating.
We'll now check her pulse and blood pressure.
Pulse is 74-strong and regular.
Blood pressure is 177 over 131 currently.
Glucose is 108; no acute issues noted at this time.
Bystander, does she have medical conditions we should know about?
Bystander, when did she last eat or drink?

dialogue.micro_intent: S.A.M.P.L.E. - events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: Bystander, when did her trouble speaking start?
Mary, where exactly is your headache?
Right behind my eyes.
We confirmed you are a 68-year-old female with no known allergies.
On a 0-10 scale, how bad is your headache?
It's a 10. I've never felt anything so severe.
How would you describe it? Is it sharp, pounding, or dull?
Pounding. Right behind the eyes.
Are you experiencing any pain beyond the headache?
No. I feel some pressure but no actual pain.

dialogue.micro_intent: mental status / Short & Long term memory assessment
dialogue.utterance: Mary, can you tell me what today's date is?

dialogue.micro_intent: mental status / Short & Long term memory assessment
dialogue.utterance: I think it's the 19th, but I'm not sure.
Can we briefly shine a light into your eyes for assessment?
Pupils are equal, round, and reactive-Pearls.
Will you permit us to place ECG leads for monitoring?
ECG started-initial rhythm is normal sinus.
Pulse is 71 now-slightly reduced from earlier.
Respirations remain steady at 18, and unlabored.
Blood pressure is back up-207 over 120.
SpO2 is 99%, stable.
We'll need to place an IV line for vascular access-your consent?
IV line placed at 18-gauge in the left arm using a saline lock.
We're following the Stroke/TIA protocol and will transport her expedited.
We'll be taking you to Johnston Willis Hospital-understood?
We will assist you to a stair chair for the secure transfer.
All safety straps are in place for support and positioning.
You are now secured safely on the chair-still comfortable?
(nods slowly)
Initial transfer from the couch completed without difficulty.
All anchor points are fastened-you're secured for the next step.
You'll be transported in semi-Fowler's position for the duration.
Respirations remain stable at 18, unchanged from earlier.
Blood pressure has decreased to 183 over 114.
Heart rate is stable at 70, maintaining a regular rhythm.
SpO2 is at 99%, consistent with prior readings.
Let me update your latest vitals.
Blood pressure is 190 over 120 now.
SpO2 is 97%.
IV line remains patent and intact.
Any new or worsening symptoms since now?
Still no other pain reported.
Speech improved slightly-still can be slow and effortful.
Mary, are you still reporting pressure-related discomfort without actual pain?
Yes, still pressure but no pain.
You are moving now-everything is secured.

dialogue.micro_intent: transfer of care to receiving facility
dialogue.utterance: Nurse on duty, this is a 68-year-old female with suspected Stroke/CVA.

dialogue.micro_intent: transfer of care to receiving facility
dialogue.utterance: Chronic conditions include diabetes, hypertension, migraines, and prior CVA.

dialogue.micro_intent: transfer of care to receiving facility
dialogue.utterance: Latest vitals: BP 190/120, P 70, O2 97%.

dialogue.micro_intent: transfer of care to receiving facility
dialogue.utterance: EKG monitoring is in place, IV started and functional.

dialogue.micro_intent: transfer of care to receiving facility
dialogue.utterance: No field-administered medications during transport.

dialogue.micro_intent: transfer of care to receiving facility
dialogue.utterance: Patient transported hot in semi-Fowlers, neurological concerns present.

dialogue.micro_intent: transfer of care to receiving facility
dialogue.utterance: Glucose levels were 108, steady over the transport.

dialogue.micro_intent: transfer of care to receiving facility
dialogue.utterance: GCS score is 14 (E4, V4, M6).

dialogue.micro_intent: transfer of care to receiving facility
dialogue.utterance: She was initially non-verbal but has been able to answer questions slowly.

dialogue.micro_intent: transfer of care to receiving facility
dialogue.utterance: History of TIA and migraines noted in chart.

dialogue.micro_intent: transfer of care to receiving facility
dialogue.utterance: Headache was reported as 10/10 with no other pain noted.
